chapter_title: Areas of Knowledge and Mathematical Methods
learning_objective: Assess the degree of certainty achievable in different Areas of Knowledge by positioning them on a spectrum and justifying their placement.
specification:
Type: diagram **sim-id:** certainty-spectrum-aok
**Library:** p5.js
**Status:** Specified **Bloom Level:** Evaluate (L5) **Bloom Verb:** Assess **Learning Objective:** Assess the degree of certainty achievable in different Areas of Knowledge by positioning them on a spectrum and justifying their placement. **Instructional Rationale:** A draggable spectrum visualization helps students move beyond the binary of "certain vs. uncertain" and develop nuanced thinking about degrees of confidence across AOKs. **Visual elements:** - A horizontal spectrum bar from "Least Certain" (left) to "Most Certain" (right) - Eight draggable AOK cards, each labeled with the AOK name and a brief description - A justification panel below the spectrum where explanations appear for each AOK's position - Guide markers at key positions: "Deductive certainty," "Strong empirical support," "Interpretive," "Value-dependent" **Interactive controls:** - Drag any AOK card to a position on the spectrum - Click on a placed card to read a 2-3 sentence justification for its position - A "Show Expert View" button that reveals how epistemologists typically rank the AOKs by certainty - A "Compare My View" mode that overlays the student's ranking with the expert ranking **Default state:** All eight AOK cards arranged randomly below the spectrum, ready to be dragged. **Color scheme:** Spectrum gradient from coral (least certain) through cream to teal (most certain). Cards in white with teal borders. **Responsive design:** Canvas resizes to fit container width. On narrow screens, spectrum switches to vertical orientation. Implementation: p5.js with drag-and-drop detection and createButton() for mode toggles.
